blocks.content:
The **DOM** (Document Object Model) is how JavaScript talks to HTML. It represents your web page as a tree of elements.

The `document` object is your gateway to the DOM:

```javascript
document.getElementById('myId')      // Find by ID
document.querySelector('.myClass')   // Find by selector
document.querySelectorAll('button')  // Find all buttons
```

blocks.title: 📝 What's Next
blocks.content: The playground below shows concepts. In real web pages, JavaScript would interact with actual HTML elements to create interactivity.

blocks.title: DOM Concepts

blocks.content:
## Selecting Elements

```javascript
// By ID (returns one element)
document.getElementById('main')
document.querySelector('#main')

// By class (returns a list or one)
document.querySelector('.btn')
document.querySelectorAll('.btn')

// By tag (returns all matching)
document.querySelectorAll('p')
```

questions.question: What does the DOM represent?
options: The structure of a web page as JavaScript can see it
questions.explanation: The DOM (Document Object Model) represents an HTML document as a tree of objects that JavaScript can access and modify.
